note: EdHermes auto-posts on gateway start to its home channel. Lifecycle broadcast, not a task reply.

hermes: ⚠️ Gateway shutting down — Your current task will be interrupted.
note: Posted on shutdown; ⚠️ prefix = warning/interruption. An in-flight run is aborted.
Direct, complete answer with no marker = a result, effectively terminal. Plain-text replies are the hard case the LLM interpreter must classify (no marker to key on).

prompt: Schedule a meeting for me tomorrow.
hermes: ❓ clarify: "I'd be happy to schedule a meeting fo..."
note: Ambiguous request → Hermes asks for more info. '❓ clarify:' is a reliable structured marker for run_blocked (needs user input). A blocked clarify HOLDS the session until answered.

prompt: Give me a two-line poem about debugging.
hermes: ⏳ Still working... (3 min elapsed — iteration 1/90, running: clarify)
note: Progress heartbeat, NOT a result. Hermes emits these every gateway_notify_interval (~180s) while a run is active; embeds elapsed time, iteration n/max_turns, and the running tool. Treat as in-progress/keepalive. Here it shows the session was still stuck on the prior 'clarify' run.
